title: Maximum Product of Two Integers With No Common Bits
performanceTarget: top 1% execution time and overall performance
note: C++ proposals for this challenge. The runtime variants combine product-pruned scanning with compressed SOS-DP fallback, avoiding both quadratic blowups and unnecessary full-domain DP on easy cases.

variants.languageReason: The default solution uses C++ sorting, branch-light bit checks, and a vector-backed compressed SOS-DP fallback. This balances practical speed with compact allocation.
variants.approachJustification: Try high-product pairs first under a fixed scan cap. If that cap is reached, compress active bit positions and compute the maximum present submask for every compressed mask.
variants.timeComplexity: O(n log n + L + r * 2^r + k), r <= 20

variants.languageReason: The runtime variant keeps the exact hybrid algorithm but uses static arrays sized to the constraints, reducing repeated allocation overhead in LeetCode's multi-testcase runner.
variants.approachJustification: Use bounded product scanning first, then a static-array compressed SOS-DP fallback initialized only over the active mask range.
variants.timeComplexity: O(n log n + L + r * 2^r + k), r <= 20

reasoningSummary: The final runtime strategy first exploits descending product pruning, then falls back to compressed SOS DP for exact complement-submask lookups. This avoids TLE from both unbounded pair scans and always-on full 2^20 DP.
timeComplexity: O(n log n + L + r * 2^r + k), r <= 20
spaceComplexity: O(2^r + k) recommended, O(2^20 + 1e5) reserved runtime
